title: Fix build origin sentinel — eliminate (0,0,0) as \"missing origin\" signal
description:
From all three audits (93-97% confidence): HtnTaskLibrary.DecomposeBuild still checks `originX == 0 && originY == 0 && originZ == 0` as the signal for auto-detect, even though BuildOrigin value object was introduced to eliminate sentinel overloading. This makes building at world origin (0,0,0) impossible — it's silently reinterpreted as "no origin supplied".

Fix: Change DecomposeBuild to accept `BuildOrigin?` instead of raw `int originX/originY/originZ`. Remove the (0,0,0) sentinel check. Use `BuildOriginSource` enum to distinguish Explicit vs AutoScanned vs Fact origins.

Files: Agent.Planning/HtnTaskLibrary.cs (DecomposeBuild), Agent.Planning/Decomposition/BuildGoalDecomposer.cs, Agent.Planning/Goals/BuildOrigin.cs

This is a larger refactor touching the build planning pipeline. Coordinate with BuildGoalDecomposer and any callers.

Source: memorysmith_agent_supplemental_audit_findings_2026-06-25.md (Finding 2), deep_code_audit (Finding 1), sprint35_audit (Findings 1-2)

comments.author: anonymous
comments.body: Deferred from Sprint 49 Wave B — requires deeper refactor threading BuildOrigin? through HtnTaskLibrary.DecomposeBuild pipeline. See handoff at Data/Pages/Handoffs/sprint-49-waveb-handoff-2026-06-25.md.
